page: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor to ensure dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

A key differentiator of the m6id family is the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage. The m6id.16xlarge includes 3800 GB of direct-attached NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-throughput, low-latency local disk access. For network connectivity, the instance delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. The hardware excels in cryptographic operations and extended instructions, supported by CPU flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, while single-core performance and memory bandwidth fall within the average range.

Given the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio combined with high-capacity local NVMe storage, this instance offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring fast local scratch space. It is highly suited for relational databases, data analytics, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving, balancing compute power with local storage performance without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated alternatives.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. While it lacks GPU acceleration, it provides a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations, alongside average single-core and memory bandwidth results. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for workloads that require a balance of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage, such as relational databases, web serving, and software development pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining 64 Intel vCPUs, DDR4 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for demanding multi-core workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Demonstrates strong multi-core CPU, database, and software compilation performance
Well-suited for relational databases, web serving, and data analytics
